addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

5044	static size_t CeilDiv(size_t a, size_t b)
5045	/* The divisor must be positive (division by zero is undefined). */
5046	__CPROVER_requires(b >= 1)
5047	/* Keep a + b - 1 from wrapping so the ceiling arithmetic is well defined. */
5048	__CPROVER_requires(a <= __CPROVER_max_malloc_size)
5049	__CPROVER_assigns()
5050	/* Exact value: the ceiling of a / b.  This pins the operator direction
5051	   (division, not multiplication) and the rounding constant (+ b - 1, not
5052	   + b + 1 or - b - 1), killing the corresponding mutants. */
5053	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value == (a + b - 1) / b)
5054	{
5055	    return (a + b - 1) / b;
5056	}
5057	
5058	void ZopfliCopyLZ77Store(
5059	    const ZopfliLZ77Store *source, ZopfliLZ77Store *dest)
5060	// clang-format off
5061	/* Deep copy of an LZ77 store: cleans/reinitialises dest, allocates seven fresh
5062	   arrays sized to source->size (and the wrap-around histograms to llsize/dsize),
5063	   then copies every element across.  This is a malloc-then-fill function with
5064	   three fill loops, so it is verified UNBOUNDED with dynamic frame-condition
5065	   checking and loop contracts (see [[zic-dfcc-loopcontracts-12of16]]); the
5066	   standard --depth 200 flow makes the loop bodies vacuous because the histogram
5067	   loop alone runs >= ZOPFLI_NUM_LL == 288 iterations.
5068	
5069	   llsize == ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * ceil(size / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) and
5070	   dsize  == ZOPFLI_NUM_D  * ceil(size / ZOPFLI_NUM_D), written out below so the
5071	   precondition can size source's histograms exactly. */
5072	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source, sizeof(*source)))
5073	/* size >= 1 makes the sampled boundary index (size-1) well defined; the upper
5074	   bound keeps every malloc / CeilDiv product from wrapping (symbolic, not a CLI
5075	   value). */
5076	__CPROVER_requires(source->size >= 1)
5077	__CPROVER_requires(source->size <= __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * sizeof(size_t)))
5078	/* source's per-symbol arrays span exactly source->size elements (loop 1). */
5079	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source->litlens, source->size * sizeof(*source->litlens)))
5080	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source->dists, source->size * sizeof(*source->dists)))
5081	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source->pos, source->size * sizeof(*source->pos)))
5082	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source->ll_symbol, source->size * sizeof(*source->ll_symbol)))
5083	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source->d_symbol, source->size * sizeof(*source->d_symbol)))
5084	/* source's wrap-around histograms span llsize / dsize (loops 2 and 3). */
5085	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source->ll_counts, (ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)) * sizeof(*source->ll_counts)))
5086	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(source->d_counts, (ZOPFLI_NUM_D * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_D - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_D)) * sizeof(*source->d_counts)))
5087	/* dest is a live store whose seven arrays the leading ZopfliCleanLZ77Store
5088	   frees; is_fresh makes each a valid (freeable) pointer. */
5089	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest, sizeof(*dest)))
5090	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->litlens, sizeof(*dest->litlens)))
5091	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->dists, sizeof(*dest->dists)))
5092	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->pos, sizeof(*dest->pos)))
5093	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->ll_symbol, sizeof(*dest->ll_symbol)))
5094	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->d_symbol, sizeof(*dest->d_symbol)))
5095	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->ll_counts, sizeof(*dest->ll_counts)))
5096	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->d_counts, sizeof(*dest->d_counts)))
5097	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(dest))
5098	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->size == source->size)
5099	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->data == source->data)
5100	/* Freshly allocated destination arrays, sized exactly to what was copied. */
5101	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->litlens, source->size * sizeof(*dest->litlens)))
5102	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->dists, source->size * sizeof(*dest->dists)))
5103	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->pos, source->size * sizeof(*dest->pos)))
5104	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->ll_symbol, source->size * sizeof(*dest->ll_symbol)))
5105	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->d_symbol, source->size * sizeof(*dest->d_symbol)))
5106	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->ll_counts, (ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)) * sizeof(*dest->ll_counts)))
5107	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_is_fresh(dest->d_counts, (ZOPFLI_NUM_D * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_D - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_D)) * sizeof(*dest->d_counts)))
5108	/* Sampled boundary content: the first and last element of each array equals its
5109	   source counterpart.  These non-quantified ensures kill the loop-bound mutants
5110	   (0-iteration variants leave the boundary element unwritten; <= variants read
5111	   one past the end). */
5112	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->litlens[0] == source->litlens[0] && dest->litlens[source->size - 1] == source->litlens[source->size - 1])
5113	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->dists[0] == source->dists[0] && dest->dists[source->size - 1] == source->dists[source->size - 1])
5114	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->pos[0] == source->pos[0] && dest->pos[source->size - 1] == source->pos[source->size - 1])
5115	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->ll_symbol[0] == source->ll_symbol[0] && dest->ll_symbol[source->size - 1] == source->ll_symbol[source->size - 1])
5116	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->d_symbol[0] == source->d_symbol[0] && dest->d_symbol[source->size - 1] == source->d_symbol[source->size - 1])
5117	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->ll_counts[0] == source->ll_counts[0] && dest->ll_counts[(ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)) - 1] == source->ll_counts[(ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_LL - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_LL)) - 1])
5118	__CPROVER_ensures(dest->d_counts[0] == source->d_counts[0] && dest->d_counts[(ZOPFLI_NUM_D * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_D - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_D)) - 1] == source->d_counts[(ZOPFLI_NUM_D * ((source->size + ZOPFLI_NUM_D - 1) / ZOPFLI_NUM_D)) - 1])
5119	// clang-format on
5120	{
5121	    size_t i;
5122	    size_t llsize = ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * CeilDiv(source->size, ZOPFLI_NUM_LL);
5123	    size_t dsize = ZOPFLI_NUM_D * CeilDiv(source->size, ZOPFLI_NUM_D);
5124	    ZopfliCleanLZ77Store(dest);
5125	    ZopfliInitLZ77Store(source->data, dest);
5126	    dest->litlens =
5127	        (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(*dest->litlens) * source->size);
5128	    dest->dists = (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(*dest->dists) * source->size);
5129	    dest->pos = (size_t *)malloc(sizeof(*dest->pos) * source->size);
5130	    dest->ll_symbol =
5131	        (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(*dest->ll_symbol) * source->size);
5132	    dest->d_symbol =
5133	        (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(*dest->d_symbol) * source->size);
5134	    dest->ll_counts = (size_t *)malloc(sizeof(*dest->ll_counts) * llsize);
5135	    dest->d_counts = (size_t *)malloc(sizeof(*dest->d_counts) * dsize);
5136	
5137	    /* Allocation failed. */
5138	    if (!dest->litlens || !dest->dists)
5139	        exit(-1);
5140	    if (!dest->pos)
5141	        exit(-1);
5142	    if (!dest->ll_symbol || !dest->d_symbol)
5143	        exit(-1);
5144	    if (!dest->ll_counts || !dest->d_counts)
5145	        exit(-1);
5146	
5147	    dest->size = source->size;
5148	    for (i = 0; i < source->size; i++)
5149	    __CPROVER_assigns(i, __CPROVER_object_whole(dest->litlens), __CPROVER_object_whole(dest->dists), __CPROVER_object_whole(dest->pos), __CPROVER_object_whole(dest->ll_symbol), __CPROVER_object_whole(dest->d_symbol))

#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=ZopfliInitCache
WORK=/app/_kill_zic
DEPTH=${1:-200}
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK

# Mutations described as (line-in-SRC, original-text, mutant-text).
# Loop-condition mutants are line-targeted (sed) so the two identical
# "i < blocksize" loops are mutated independently.
LINES=(3614 3624 3624 3624 3624 3624 3631 3631 3631 3631 3631 3638 3638 3638 3638 3638)
ORIG=(
'    if (lmc->sublen == NULL)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
)
MUT=(
'    if (lmc->sublen != NULL)'
'    for (i = 0; i <= blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i > blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i >= blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i == blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i != blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i <= blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i > blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i >= blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i == blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i != blocksize; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i <= ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i > ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i >= ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i == ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
'    for (i = 0; i != ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * blocksize * 3; i++)'
)

killed=0
for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto md.goto ml.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  L="${LINES[$idx]}"; O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"
  # sanity: line matches expected original
  if [ "$(sed -n "${L}p" mutated.c)" != "$O" ]; then echo "LINEMISS $idx (line $L)"; continue; fi
  M="$M" perl -i -pe "s/.*/\$ENV{M}/ if \$. == $L" mutated.c
  if [ "$(sed -n "${L}p" mutated.c)" != "$M" ]; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto mutated.c /app/_zic_harness.c /app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function __zic_harness 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "CCFAIL $idx"; cat /tmp/cc.err; continue; fi
  goto-instrument --dfcc __zic_harness --enforce-contract $FUNCTION --apply-loop-contracts m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function __zic_harness 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx: $M"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="

---
name: zic-dfcc-loopcontracts-12of16
description: "ZopfliInitCache hit 12/16 via dfcc loop contracts + alloc stub + non-quantified boundary invariants, beating depth-200 vacuity"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 61358d05-5218-424b-a7ac-a9564dd0ffae
---

ZopfliInitCache (3 malloc+fill loops over blocksize) scores **12/16** — far better than
the usual depth-200-vacuity 0-kill outcome for loop-heavy Zopfli funcs. The technique
(novel in this repo; reusable for other malloc-then-fill funcs):

**Flow (NOT the standard `--depth 200`):** verify UNBOUNDED with dynamic frame condition
checking + loop contracts. `--depth 200` is what makes loop bodies vacuous; loop contracts
abstract the loops so no depth bound is needed.
```
goto-cc src.c HARNESS.c /app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c --function __harness
goto-instrument --dfcc __harness --enforce-contract FN --apply-loop-contracts in.goto out.goto
cbmc out.goto --function __harness            # no --depth
```
- Need a separate HARNESS function (mallocs the struct, calls FN) — dfcc requires harness_id != contract fn.
- MUST link /app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c, else dfcc's contracts library hits "no body for free" → `Numeric exception` crash.
- Plain `--apply-loop-contracts` (non-dfcc) does NOT track locally-malloc'd arrays as writable in loop frames → assigns failures. dfcc does.

**Quantified loop invariants DON'T WORK in this CBMC.** Even the canonical array-init example
(`forall j. j<i ==> arr[j]==0`) fails loop_invariant_step: whole-array `object_whole` havoc
+ assumed `forall` isn't instantiated at the checked index. Don't chase quantified content.

**Workaround that gives the kills — NON-quantified boundary invariants/postconditions:**
- ensures: `arr[0]==v && arr[n-1]==v` (sampled first+last, not forall).
- loop invariants: `i<=n`, `__CPROVER_w_ok(arr, size)`, `(i>0)==>(arr[0]==v)`,
  `(i>=n)==>(arr[n-1]==v)`. These preserve WITHOUT quantifier instantiation.
- loop assigns: `__CPROVER_assigns(i, __CPROVER_object_whole(arr))`.
- requires `blocksize <= __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (per-elem-bytes)` to keep CAR/malloc
  size assertions valid (symbolic, not a hardcoded CLI value).

This kills: `<=` (OOB caught by w_ok bounds), `>`/`>=`/`==` (0-iter → boundary elem unwritten
→ postcondition fails). Survivors are inherent: 3 `!=` equivalent mutants (0-start +1-step
loop ≡ `<`) + 1 `sublen!=NULL` (never-NULL malloc stub → mutant always exits → ensures vacuous).

Scripts: /app/_verify_zic.sh, /app/kill_zic.sh, /app/_zic_harness.c. Contrast [[avocado-depth200-vacuity]].

name: zcopylz-depth200-vacuity
description: "ZopfliCopyLZ77Store verifies (official PASS) but 0/18 kills under the official --depth-200 grader; loop 2 always >=288 iters, inherent vacuity"
metadata:
  node_type: memory
  type: project
---

ZopfliCopyLZ77Store (zopfli.c ~5058): deep-copy, 3 malloc-then-fill loops over
source->size, llsize, dsize. Already carries a strong dfcc-style spec (is_fresh
preconditions on all 7 source + 7 dest arrays, object_whole assigns, sampled
boundary `[0]`/`[n-1]` ensures, and per-loop `__CPROVER_loop_invariant` /
`__CPROVER_assigns` / `w_ok` — a copy of the [[zic-dfcc-loopcontracts-12of16]]
technique).

**It verifies:** `tools.run_cbmc` → PASS.

**Kill score under the OFFICIAL grader = 0/18 (all survive).** The official
`run_cbmc` flow is `--depth 200` WITHOUT loop contracts — proven decisively: a
mutant the [[zic-dfcc-loopcontracts-12of16]] note records as KILLED under the
manual dfcc flow (`ZopfliInitCache` loop `i < blocksize` → `i <= blocksize`)
ALSO SURVIVES under `run_cbmc`. So the loop-contract annotations are inert to the
grader; the loops just truncate at depth 200.

**Why 0 is inherent here (worse than zic):** loop 2 (ll_counts) runs
llsize = ZOPFLI_NUM_LL * ceil(size/ZOPFLI_NUM_LL) >= 288 iterations ALWAYS
(ZOPFLI_NUM_LL==288, ceil>=1). Under --depth 200 that loop never completes, so
loop 3 and EVERY final `__CPROVER_ensures` (incl. loop-1's boundary ensures, which
sit at function exit after loop 2) are unreachable → vacuous. Pinning source->size
small does NOT help: loop 2 is still >=288. The 18 mutants:
- 15 loop-bound (`<=`,`>`,`>=`,`==`,`!=` on each of 3 loops): 12 killable only via
  the dfcc flow; under --depth 200 all masked by loop-2 non-completion. The 3 `!=`
  are equivalent mutants regardless (0-start/+1-step ≡ `<`).
- 3 alloc-check `||`→`&&` (lines 5138/5142/5144): EQUIVALENT — the never-NULL
  malloc stub (/app/stubs/cprover_alloc.c) makes both branches dead.

So ceiling under the grader is 0; ceiling under the manual dfcc flow is 12/18
(4 killable per loop × 3, minus 3 `!=` + 3 `||→&&` equivalents). The spec is
already maximal for the dfcc technique. Don't chase. Same class as
[[zslld-depth200-vacuity]], [[zuh-depth200-vacuity]], [[silmc-depth200-vacuity]],
[[zstc-depth200-vacuity]].

Scripts: /app/_verify_zcopylz.sh + /app/_zclz_harness.c (dfcc flow, SLOW: original
>10min — 7 arrays), /app/_kill_zcopylz.py (official run_cbmc kill harness → 0/18).
